I'll create a React dashboard frontend and implement all 20 AI modules, auth, real-time alerts, and dashboards in the existing Node.js/Express backend (adapting the Python spec to the established workspace stack).

Let me read the backend references first, then scaffold everything.

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on a snippet of .local/skills/pnpm-workspace/references/openapi.md
     1→# OpenAPI Spec & Codegen
     2→
     3→`lib/api-spec/openapi.yaml` is the single source of truth for all API contracts. Define endpoints here first, then generate typed helpers.
     4→
     5→## Codegen
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→pnpm --filter @workspace/api-spec run codegen
     9→```
    10→
    11→This produces:
    12→
    13→- Zod validation schemas in `@workspace/api-zod` (used by the server)
    14→- React Query hooks in `@workspace/api-client-react` (used by frontends)
    15→
    16→Re-run codegen after every spec change. Do not hand-write types that the codegen already produces.
    17→
    18→After codegen, Orval writes to fixed paths:
    19→
    20→```text
    21→lib/api-client-react/src/generated/api.ts
    22→lib/api-client-react/src/generated/api.schemas.ts
    23→lib/api-zod/src/generated/api.ts
    24→```
    25→
    26→## Writing the spec
    27→
    28→- Base server URL is `/api`. Do not change this.
    29→- Keep the existing `/healthz` endpoint and `HealthStatus` schema.
    30→- Every endpoint must have an `operationId` — Orval uses these to derive hook and schema names.
    31→- Put every request/update body in `components/schemas` and `$ref` it from the operation. Use entity-shaped names (`NoteInput`, `NoteUpdate`, `ItemInput`) — never operation-shaped names like `CreateNoteBody` or `UpdateNoteBody`, and never inline the body. See "Body schema naming" below.
    32→- For array responses, define the item schema separately under `components/schemas` and reference it with `type: array` + `items: { $ref: ... }`.
    33→- For nullable fields use `type: ["<actual_type>", "null"]` (OpenAPI 3.1), where `<actual_type>` is the real type of the field (`string`, `integer`, `number`, `boolean`, etc.). For example: `type: ["integer", "null"]` for a nullable number.
    34→- The workspace Orval config forces `info.title` to `Api` so generated outputs stay at the fixed `generated/api*` filenames. Do not fight that convention or hand-rename the generated files/barrels.
    35→
    36→## Body schema naming (avoid TS2308 collisions)
    37→
    38→Every request/update body **must** live in `components/schemas` and be `$ref`'d from the operation. The component name **must not** match `<OperationIdPascal>Body` (the name Orval auto-derives). Use entity-shaped names (`NoteInput`, `NoteUpdate`, `ItemInput`).
    39→
    40→Why: For each operation with a body, Orval emits a Zod schema to `generated/api.ts` named `<OperationIdPascal>Body` (e.g. `createNote` → `CreateNoteBody`). It separately emits TypeScript interfaces for everything reachable as a body schema into `generated/types/`. The `lib/api-zod` barrel re-exports both with `export *`. If those two names collide, you get:
    41→
    42→```text
    43→error TS2308: Module "./generated/api" has already exported a member named 'CreateNoteBody'.
    44→```
    45→
    46→The build fails after codegen during `pnpm -w run typecheck:libs`. Orval itself succeeds — only the chained typecheck blows up, so the failure looks like a codegen error but is really a downstream typecheck error.
    47→
    48→The collision happens in two ways, both avoidable:
    49→
    50→1. **Inline body** — Orval invents `<OperationIdPascal>Body` as the TS type name for the inline shape and emits it to `generated/types/`. Always collides with the Zod schema of the same name in `api.ts`.
    51→2. **`$ref` to a component named `<OperationIdPascal>Body`** — same name in both files. Same collision.
    52→
    53→The single rule that avoids both: **`$ref` every body, and name the component after the entity, not the operation.**
    54→
    55→### Right pattern
    56→
    57→```yaml
    58→paths:
    59→  /notes:
    60→    post:
    61→      operationId: createNote
    62→      requestBody:
    63→        required: true
    64→        content:
    65→          application/json:
    66→            schema:
    67→              $ref: "#/components/schemas/NoteInput"   # entity-shaped name
    68→      responses:
    69→        "201":
    70→          description: Created
    71→          content:
    72→            application/json:
    73→              schema:
    74→                $ref: "#/components/schemas/Note"
    75→    patch:
    76→      operationId: updateNote
    77→      requestBody:
    78→        required: true
    79→        content:
    80→          application/json:
    81→            schema:
    82→              $ref: "#/components/schemas/NoteUpdate"   # entity-shaped name
    83→
    84→components:
    85→  schemas:
    86→    Note:                  # domain entity (reused in responses)
    87→      type: object
    88→      required: [id, title]
    89→      properties:
    90→        id:    { type: integer }
    91→        title: { type: string }
    92→        body:  { type: ["string", "null"] }
    93→    NoteInput:             # body schema (entity-shaped)
    94→      type: object
    95→      required: [title]
    96→      properties:
    97→        title: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
    98→        body:  { type: string }
    99→    NoteUpdate:
   100→      type: object
   101→      properties:
   102→        title: { type: string, minLength: 1 }
   103→        body:  { type: string }
   104→```
   105→
   106→### Wrong patterns (cause TS2308)
   107→
   108→```yaml
   109→# (a) Operation-shaped component name
   110→components:
   111→  schemas:
   112→    CreateNoteBody: { ... }   # collides with api.ts CreateNoteBody
   113→    UpdateNoteBody: { ... }   # collides with api.ts UpdateNoteBody
   114→
   115→# (b) Inline body — Orval still invents CreateNoteBody in types/
   116→paths:
   117→  /notes:
   118→    post:
   119→      operationId: createNote
   120→      requestBody:
   121→        content:
   122→          application/json:
   123→            schema:
   124→              type: object   # inline — collides
   125→              properties: { title: { type: string } }
   126→```
   127→
   128→## Using generated hooks
   129→
   130→**Queries** return data typed as `T` directly — no wrapper object around the payload. Each query hook also exports a `get*QueryKey` helper for cache invalidation:
   131→
   132→```tsx
   133→import { useGetNotes, getGetNotesQueryKey } from "@workspace/api-client-react";
   134→
   135→const { data, isLoading } = useGetNotes();
   136→// data is Note[] directly
   137→
   138→// After a mutation, invalidate the cache:
   139→queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: getGetNotesQueryKey() });
   140→```
   141→
   142→**Mutations** take variables as `{ data: T }` (plus any path params). The `onSuccess` result is typed as `T` directly:
   143→
   144→```tsx
   145→const mutation = useCreateNote();
   146→mutation.mutate({ data: { title: "New note" } });
   147→```
   148→
   149→**Errors** are `ApiError` instances with `status`, `statusText`, `data`, and `headers` properties — these fields live on the error object, not the success response.
   150→
   151→## Errors
   152→
   153→- Use standard HTTP status codes (400, 404) for errors
   154→- Generated React Query hooks handle error/loading states automatically
   155→- Keep error responses simple, for example `{ error: "message" }`
   156→
   157→## Deriving endpoints
   158→
   159→Before writing the spec, list every user-facing action the app supports, then map each to an endpoint:
   160→
   161→```text
   162→Features:
   163→- Users can create, view, edit, delete notes
   164→- Notes can be favorited
   165→
   166→Derived endpoints:
   167→  GET    /notes           → list
   168→  POST   /notes           → create
   169→  GET    /notes/{id}      → get
   170→  PATCH  /notes/{id}      → update
   171→  DELETE /notes/{id}      → delete
   172→  PATCH  /notes/{id}/star → toggleFavorite
   173→```
   174→
   175→If a feature implies a user action, it needs an endpoint in the spec. Missing endpoints mean missing generated hooks, which means the frontend can't call them.
   176→
   177→## If delegating spec to a subagent
   178→
   179→Tell the subagent:
   180→
   181→- The exact file path: `lib/api-spec/openapi.yaml`
   182→- The codegen command: `pnpm --filter @workspace/api-spec run codegen`
   183→- To keep the existing `HealthStatus` schema and `/healthz` endpoint
   184→- Every schema and endpoint in detail — don't leave anything for the subagent to infer
   185→

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on a snippet of .local/skills/pnpm-workspace/references/db.md
     1→# Database (Drizzle ORM)
     2→
     3→## Schema location
     4→
     5→Each table goes in its own file under `lib/db/src/schema/`. Re-export from `lib/db/src/schema/index.ts`.
     6→
     7→## Pattern
     8→
     9→Every model file should define: the Drizzle table, an insert schema, and the derived types.
    10→
    11→```typescript
    12→import { pgTable, text, serial, timestamp } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
    13→import { createInsertSchema } from "drizzle-zod";
    14→import { z } from "zod/v4";
    15→
    16→export const todosTable = pgTable("todos", {
    17→  id: serial("id").primaryKey(),
    18→  title: text("title").notNull(),
    19→  completed: text("completed").notNull().default("false"),
    20→  createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
    21→  updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow().$onUpdate(() => new Date()),
    22→});
    23→
    24→export const insertTodoSchema = createInsertSchema(todosTable).omit({ id: true, createdAt: true, updatedAt: true });
    25→export type InsertTodo = z.infer<typeof insertTodoSchema>;
    26→export type Todo = typeof todosTable.$inferSelect;
    27→```
    28→
    29→## Importing
    30→
    31→The `@workspace/db` package exports both the client and the full `src/schema` barrel, so prefer importing tables directly from `@workspace/db`:
    32→
    33→```typescript
    34→import { db } from "@workspace/db";
    35→import { todosTable } from "@workspace/db";
    36→```
    37→
    38→## Push commands
    39→
    40→```bash
    41→pnpm --filter @workspace/db run push
    42→# If it fails with column conflicts:
    43→pnpm --filter @workspace/db run push-force
    44→```
    45→
    46→## Pitfalls
    47→
    48→- Array columns: call `.array()` as a method on the column type — `text("tags").array()`, not `array(text("tags"))`.
    49→- Do not edit `drizzle.config.ts` unless absolutely necessary.
    50→- Make sure date fields use timestamptz by adding `{ withTimezone: true }`
    51→

openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  # Do not change the title, if the title changes, the import paths will be broken
  title: Api
  version: 0.1.0
  description: Aikya Sentinel – Unified AI Financial Crime Intelligence & Insider Threat Early Warning System API
servers:
  - url: /api
    description: Base API path
tags:
  - name: health
    description: Health operations
  - name: auth
    description: Authentication operations
  - name: users
    description: User management
  - name: employees
    description: Employee management
  - name: accounts
    description: Account management
  - name: transactions
    description: Transaction management
  - name: alerts
    description: Alert management
  - name: cases
    description: Case management
  - name: ai
    description: AI analysis modules
  - name: dashboard
    description: Dashboard and analytics

paths:
  /healthz:
    get:
      operationId: healthCheck
      tags: [health]
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthStatus"

  # Auth
  /auth/register:
    post:
      operationId: register
      tags: [auth]
      summary: Register a new user
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/UserRegisterInput"
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/AuthTokens"
        "400":
          description: Bad request

  /auth/login:
    post:
      operationId: login
      tags: [auth]
      summary: Login
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/LoginInput"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/AuthTokens"
        "401":
          description: Unauthorized

  /auth/logout:
    post:
      operationId: logout
      tags: [auth]
      summary: Logout
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/MessageResponse"

  /auth/refresh:
    post:
      operationId: refreshToken
      tags: [auth]
      summary: Refresh access token
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/RefreshTokenInput"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/AuthTokens"

  /auth/me:
    get:
      operationId: getMe
      tags: [auth]
      summary: Get current user
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/User"

  # Users
  /users:
    get:
      operationId: listUsers
      tags: [users]
      summary: List users
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/User"
    post:
      operationId: createUser
      tags: [users]
      summary: Create user
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/UserInput"
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/User"

  /users/{id}:
    get:
      operationId: getUser
      tags: [users]
      summary: Get user
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/User"
        "404":
          description: Not found
    patch:
      operationId: updateUser
      tags: [users]
      summary: Update user
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/UserUpdate"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/User"
    delete:
      operationId: deleteUser
      tags: [users]
      summary: Delete user
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        "204":
          description: No content

  # Employees
  /employees:
    get:
      operationId: listEmployees
      tags: [employees]
      summary: List employees
      responses:
        "200":
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/Employee"
    post:
      operationId: createEmployee
      tags: [employees]
      summary: Create employee
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:

components:
  schemas:
    HealthStatus:
      type: object
      required: [status]

        status:
          type: string

      type: object
      required: [message]
      properties:
        message:
          type: string

        refreshToken:
          type: string
        user:

    UserRegisterInput:
      type: object
      required: [name, email, password, role]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        password:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string
          enum: [admin, compliance_officer, investigator, risk_analyst]

    LoginInput:
      type: object
      required: [email, password]
      properties:
        email:
          type: string
        password:
          type: string

    RefreshTokenInput:
      type: object
      required: [refreshToken]
      properties:
        refreshToken:
          type: string

    User:
      type: object
      required: [id, name, email, role, createdAt]
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string

    UserInput:
      type: object
      required: [name, email, password, role]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        password:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string

          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        role:
          type: string

    Employee:

          type: string
        department:

        designation:

        trustScore:
          type: number

     1→## Frontend
     2→
     3→- Use `wouter` for routing on the frontend.
     4→  - If you need to add a new page, add them to the `src/pages` directory and register them in `src/App.tsx`.
     5→  - If there are multiple pages, use a sidebar for navigation. Use the `Link` component or the `useLocation` hook from `wouter` instead of modifying the window directly.
     6→- For forms, always use shadcn's `useForm` hook and `Form` component from `@/components/ui/form` which wraps `react-hook-form`.
     7→  - When appropriate, use the `zodResolver` from `@hookform/resolvers/zod` to validate form data.
     8→  - Use `.extend` to add validation rules to the schema.
     9→  - Remember that the form component is controlled, ensure you pass default values to the `useForm` hook.
    10→- Use the Orval-generated hooks from `@workspace/api-client-react` for all API calls. Do not write raw `fetch` or manual TanStack Query hooks when generated hooks exist.
    11→  - Generated query hooks return `T` directly — NOT wrapped in `{ data: T }`.
    12→  - When passing options like `enabled`, you MUST also pass `queryKey`:
    13→
    14→    ```typescript
    15→    const { data } = useGetNote(noteId, { query: { enabled: !!noteId, queryKey: getGetNoteQueryKey(noteId) } });
    16→    ```
    17→
    18→  - For mutations, `onSuccess` receives `T` directly:
    19→
    20→    ```typescript
    21→    createRoom.mutate({ data: { name } }, {
    22→      onSuccess: (room) => { setLocation(`/room/${room.id}`); }
    23→    });
    24→    ```
    25→
    26→  - Invalidate related queries after mutations using the generated queryKey helpers.
    27→  - Show a loading or skeleton state while queries (via `.isLoading`) or mutations (via `.isPending`) are being made.
    28→- Common pitfalls to avoid:
    29→  - The `useToast` hook is exported from `@/hooks/use-toast`.
    30→  - If a form is failing to submit, try logging out `form.formState.errors` to see if there are form validation errors for fields that might not have associated form fields.
    31→  - DO NOT explicitly import React as the existing Vite setup has a JSX transformer that does it automatically.
    32→  - Use `import.meta.env.<ENV_VAR>` to access environment variables on the frontend instead of `process.env.<ENV_VAR>`. Note that variables must be prefixed with `VITE_` in order for the env vars to be available on the frontend.
    33→  - `<SelectItem>` will throw an error if it has no value prop. Provide a value prop like this `<SelectItem value="option1">`.
    34→  - NEVER import something you don't use — TypeScript will fail the build on unused imports.
    35→  - Always import React hooks (`useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef`, `useCallback`, `useMemo`) from `"react"` — never from a router library like `"wouter"`.
    36→- Add a `data-testid` attribute to every HTML element that users can interact with (buttons, inputs, links, etc.) and to elements displaying meaningful information (user data, status messages, dynamic content, key values).
    37→  - Use unique, descriptive identifiers following this pattern:
    38→    - Interactive elements: `{action}-{target}` (e.g., `button-submit`, `input-email`, `link-profile`)
    39→    - Display elements: `{type}-{content}` (e.g., `text-username`, `img-avatar`, `status-payment`)
    40→  - For dynamically generated elements (lists, grids, repeated components), append a unique identifier at the end: `{type}-{description}-{id}`
    41→    - Examples: `card-product-${productId}`, `row-user-${index}`, `text-price-${itemId}`
    42→    - The dynamic identifier can be any unique value (database ID, index, key) as long as it's unique within that group
    43→  - Keep test IDs stable and descriptive of the element's purpose rather than its appearance or implementation details.
    44→
    45→## Styling and Theming
    46→
    47→- CSS custom properties use space-separated HSL values (no `hsl()` wrapper):
    48→
    49→  ```css
    50→  :root { --primary: 221 83% 53%; }
    51→  ```
    52→
    53→  Usage: `background-color: hsl(var(--primary));`
    54→- The scaffold's `index.css` ships with every CSS custom property set to `red` as a placeholder. Rewrite the `:root` and `.dark` blocks with a cohesive theme palette before writing any components. If any `red` values remain, the page will render with red backgrounds, borders, and text.
    55→- When adding Google Fonts or any `@import url(...)` to index.css, it MUST be the very first line of the file — before `@import "tailwindcss"` and all other statements. PostCSS will fail silently if any `@import url(...)` appears after other CSS rules.
    56→- Use the `@`-prefixed paths to import shadcn components and hooks.
    57→- Use icons from `lucide-react` to signify actions and provide visual cues. Use `react-icons/si` for company logos.
    58→- User may attach assets (images, etc.) in their request.
    59→  - If the user asks you to include attached assets in the app, you can reference them in the frontend with the `@assets/...` import syntax.
    60→  - For example, if the user attached asset is at `attached_assets/example.png`, you can reference it in the frontend with `import examplePngPath from "@assets/example.png"`.
    61→
    62→## Dark Mode
    63→
    64→1. Set `darkMode: ["class"]` in tailwind.config.ts and define color variables in `:root` and `.dark` CSS classes
    65→2. Create ThemeProvider with useState("light"), useEffect to toggle "dark" class on document.documentElement, and localStorage sync
    66→3. When not using utility class names configured in `tailwind.config.ts`, always use explicit light/dark variants for ALL visual properties: `className="bg-white dark:bg-black text-black dark:text-white"`. When using utility classes configured in tailwind config, you can assume these already been configured to automatically adapt to dark mode.

await startAsyncSubagent({
    specialization: "DESIGN",
    task: `Build the complete frontend for Aikya Sentinel — a professional AI-powered financial crime intelligence and insider threat detection platform used by compliance officers, investigators, and risk analysts at financial institutions.

**Product identity:** Aikya Sentinel is where elite financial crime investigators work. Every feature is mission-critical. The UI should feel like a high-stakes intelligence cockpit — information-dense, authoritative, and precise. Users spend hours here tracking fraud, AML violations, insider threats, and suspicious fund flows. Dense but organized, every pixel earns its place. Think dark-mode-first command center with purposeful color coding for risk levels (GREEN/AMBER/RED/BLACK tiers).

**Vibe:** Tight, fast, and commanding — like a security operations center. Dark background, deliberate accent colors drawn from a deep blue/teal intelligence palette with red/amber/green for risk severity. Make a bold, deliberate color choice that someone would remember after closing the tab.

**Pages and routes:**

- \`/\` → Dashboard (redirect to /dashboard)
- \`/dashboard\` — M20 Risk Heatmap Overview: active alerts count, critical alerts, open cases, high-risk accounts/employees, flagged transactions, risk heatmap grid, recent alerts list, risk trend charts (line charts), alert stats (pie/bar), transaction volume chart
- \`/alerts\` — Alert Management: searchable/filterable alert list by severity (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) and status (OPEN/INVESTIGATING/RESOLVED/DISMISSED), alert detail drawer, update alert status, prioritise alerts
- \`/cases\` — Case Management: case list with status/priority, create case form, update case status/assignment, case detail view
- \`/transactions\` — Transaction Monitor: transaction list with filtering, flagged indicator, transaction detail
- \`/accounts\` — Account Risk View: account list with KYC status and risk score, account detail with risk scores
- \`/employees\` — Employee Risk View: employee list with trust scores, department breakdown
- \`/ai/behaviour\` — M1 Behaviour DNA Engine: analyse form, show behaviour_score, risk_level, signals, anomalies
- \`/ai/insider\` — M2 Insider Threat Engine: analyse form, show insider_risk score, risk_level, indicators
- \`/ai/fundflow\` — M3 Fund Flow Intelligence: account analysis form, show detected patterns (circular routing, layering, structuring, dormant activation), interactive graph of nodes/edges
- \`/ai/riskradar\` — M10 Risk Radar: entity analysis form, show aggregated fraud/aml/behaviour/tax/network/overall scores with radar chart, tier badge (GREEN/AMBER/RED/BLACK)
- \`/ai/xai\` — M11 Explainable AI: entity analysis + model type form, SHAP-like bar chart of feature importances, explanation text, top features
- \`/ai/timeline\` — M12 Timeline Tracker: entity timeline viewer, chronological event list with risk impact badges
- \`/ai/copilot\` — M16 Investigator Copilot: chat interface with the AI copilot, suggested actions, sources
- \`/ai/simulation\` — M17 Fraud Simulation Mode: scenario selector (AML/Insider Threat/Collusion), run simulation, show events timeline and alerts triggered
- \`/ai/reports\` — M15 Investigation Narrative Generator: generate investigation report form (entity + report type), show narrative/findings/recommendations, list past reports
- \`/settings\` — Settings: user management table, current user info

**Data types:**
- User: id, name, email, role (admin/compliance_officer/investigator/risk_analyst), createdAt
- Employee: id, employeeId, department, designation, trustScore, riskLevel, createdAt
- Account: id, accountId, customerName, kycStatus, riskScore, accountType, balance, createdAt
- Transaction: id, transactionId, sourceAccount, destinationAccount, amount, currency, transactionType, status, flagged, timestamp
- Alert: id, alertId, severity (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL), category, status, entityId, entityType, description, riskScore, createdAt
- Case: id, caseId, title, description, assignedTo, status (OPEN/IN_PROGRESS/CLOSED/ESCALATED), priority, entityId, createdAt
- RiskRadarResult: entityId, fraudScore, amlScore, behaviourScore, taxScore, networkScore, overallScore, tier

**Available API hooks (use ALL of them):**
\`\`\`
// Auth
useLogin, useLogout, useRegister, useRefreshToken
useGetMe, getGetMeQueryKey

// Users  
useListUsers, getListUsersQueryKey
useCreateUser, useUpdateUser, useDeleteUser
useGetUser, getGetUserQueryKey

// Employees
useListEmployees, getListEmployeesQueryKey
useCreateEmployee, useUpdateEmployee, useDeleteEmployee
useGetEmployee, getGetEmployeeQueryKey

// Accounts
useListAccounts, getListAccountsQueryKey
useCreateAccount, useUpdateAccount, useDeleteAccount
useGetAccount, getGetAccountQueryKey

// Transactions
useListTransactions, getListTransactionsQueryKey
useCreateTransaction
useGetTransaction, getGetTransactionQueryKey

// Alerts
useListAlerts, getListAlertsQueryKey
useCreateAlert, useUpdateAlert
useGetAlert, getGetAlertQueryKey
usePrioritiseAlerts

// Cases
useListCases, getListCasesQueryKey
useCreateCase, useUpdateCase
useGetCase, getGetCaseQueryKey

// AI Modules
useAnalyseBehaviour
useAnalyseInsiderThreat
useAnalyseFundFlow
useAnalyseTaxRisk
useAnalyseShellMule
useAnalyseCollusion
useAnalyseFraudIntent
useAnalyseDigitalTwin
useMatchPatterns
useAnalyseRiskRadar
useExplainDecision
useFetchEntityTimeline, getFetchEntityTimelineQueryKey
useCalculateTrustScore
useGenerateReport
useListReports, getListReportsQueryKey
useCopilotChat
useRunSimulation
useAnalysePersonality
useSubmitLearningFeedback

// Dashboard
useGetDashboardOverview, getGetDashboardOverviewQueryKey
useGetRiskTrends, getGetRiskTrendsQueryKey
useGetTopRiskyEntities, getGetTopRiskyEntitiesQueryKey
useGetAlertStats, getGetAlertStatsQueryKey
useGetTransactionVolume, getGetTransactionVolumeQueryKey
useGetRiskScores, getGetRiskScoresQueryKey
\`\`\`

**Hook usage rules:**
- Import all hooks from \`@workspace/api-client-react\`, never from relative paths
- Query hooks return T directly (not wrapped)
- Query hook options pattern: \`useGetThing(id, { query: { enabled: !!id, queryKey: getGetThingQueryKey(id) } })\`
- For mutations: \`mutation.mutate({ data: { ... } })\`
- Show loading/skeleton states while isLoading or isPending
- Use recharts for all charts (already installed)

**Key implementation notes:**
- Use lucide-react for all icons
- Use the sidebar component from @/components/ui/sidebar for the main navigation
- Use shadcn Card, Badge, Table, Dialog, Sheet, Form components throughout
- Risk severity colors: CRITICAL = red, HIGH = amber/orange, MEDIUM = yellow, LOW = green/blue
- Tier colors: BLACK = red-900, RED = red-600, AMBER = amber-500, GREEN = emerald-500
- The \`/ai/copilot\` page should be a proper chat UI with message history
- The \`/ai/fundflow\` graph can use a simple SVG-based force-directed visualization or recharts
- Do not use emojis anywhere in the UI
- Supporting screens (404, loading states, empty states) deserve the same design quality as main pages

**Auth:** No login page needed — assume the user is authenticated. The \`useGetMe\` hook can fetch current user info for display in the header/sidebar.

Prioritize clarity and polish. Users spend hours here — reward that with thoughtful details. Dense but organized, not sparse. Make a deliberate color choice. Don't play it safe.`,
